_note: Round-9 follow-up #3: a bare expression-statement `createEventDispatcher<{...}>()` (no `const x = …` assignment) should NOT contribute to the events surface. Upstream's `processInstanceScriptContent.ts:271` only registers dispatchers reachable as a callable identifier — bare calls have no later `dispatch('name', …)` reachable so `setEventDispatcher` never fires. Pre-fix native scanned both VarDecl AND ExpressionStatement in `find_dispatcher_event_type_sources`, so a bare call would synthesise a bogus events surface AND would have disqualified the runes fn-component shape pre-round-8 #4 (and now pre-round-9 the dispatcher type-arg would have leaked into `synthesized_typed_events`). Post-fix native walks only VarDecl declarators with BindingIdentifier names, AND recurses through Function/Block/If bodies (matching `has_event_dispatcher_call`'s recursion) so nested declarations are covered. This Producer's bare `createEventDispatcher<{phantom: string}>()` is correctly ignored; Producer stays on the fn-component shape and Consumer's `(typeof Producer)[]` type-checks clean.
